news.title: Prayagraj development body plans project management unit for streamlined urban projects
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority (PDA) is establishing a Project Management Unit (PMU) to ensure integrated planning for residential schemes. This unit will incorporate essential infrastructure like sewer lines and roads from the initial planning stages, aiming to accelerate development and minimize technical lapses.

news.title: Office leasing rises 15% to 18.3 million sq ft across seven cities in Q1 2026: Colliers
news.para: Office leasing in India's top cities will see a 15% rise to 18.3 million sq ft in Q1 2026. Technology, BFSI, and global capability centers are driving this growth. Bengaluru and Hyderabad will lead leasing activity. Flexible workspace demand is also increasing significantly. New office supply remains robust, and vacancy levels are expected to decline, supporting rental increases.

news.title: Housing sales dip 7% quarter-on-quarter in Q1 2026 amid global uncertainty: Report
news.para: Housing sales in India's top cities saw a 7% dip in the first quarter of 2026. This sequential decline occurred despite a year-on-year increase in demand. Global uncertainties and rising costs impacted buyer sentiment. New launches, however, remained resilient, exceeding sales volumes. Unsold inventory levels also rose. Residential prices continued to firm up across major urban centers.

news.title: Lucknow civic body office hours extended till March 31 for tax deposit
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation has extended working hours for its zonal offices, including Sunday and late into the night on March 30th and 31st, to boost commercial and house tax collection. This initiative aims to encourage taxpayers to clear dues before the financial year ends to avoid a 12% interest penalty on outstanding amounts.

news.title: Two construction firm directors in Mumbai held for duping NRI of ₹10 crore
news.para: Two directors of Ajanta Airawat Infrastructure LLP have been arrested. They are accused of cheating an NRI investor of Rs 10.5 crore. The investor had put in Rs 1.5 crore in 2012. Later, he extended a Rs 6 crore loan. A settlement agreed to repay Rs 10.5 crore with interest. The firm failed to pay or allot flats.

news.title: Inaugurated in haste? Wayanad township project slows to a crawl
news.para: Newly built houses for Wayanad landslide survivors remain unoccupied. Inaugurated nearly a month ago, pending plumbing and sewage works prevent families from moving in. The delay has ignited political debate, with the opposition accusing the government of an election stunt. The project faces worker shortages as migrant laborers return home for elections.

news.title: Hyderabad civic body locks over 100 big properties over tax dues
news.para: Hyderabad civic body is cracking down on major property tax defaulters, sealing over 100 commercial properties to recover hundreds of crores. High-profile establishments like Katriya Hotel and Aurora College face significant liabilities. The drive now includes vacant lands, with warnings of property auctions for non-payment. A One-Time Settlement scheme offers a 90% interest waiver.

news.title: Over 1,000 flats to be made in Prayagraj for urban poor under PMAY
news.para: Prayagraj authorities are set to build over 1,000 flats under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana Phase II. The Prayagraj Development Authority is identifying land for the project. Several locations are shortlisted, and land allocation is expected soon. This initiative will provide housing for economically weaker sections. Nearly 1,000 beneficiaries have already registered online for these upcoming homes.

news.title: Durg tops PMAY-Gramin rankings, Bijapur still at bottom of list
news.para: Durg district has secured the top position in the latest Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin performance report. The district achieved an impressive average score of 89.5%. Surajpur and Raipur follow in second and third places. Koriya district showed significant improvement, jumping three ranks. Southern districts are showing lower performance levels. Bijapur remains at the bottom.

news.title: Pune: Citizen groups to approach police to initiate FIRs against builders violating water affidavits
news.para: Pune citizen groups will file FIRs against developers who violated water supply affidavits during building permission applications. This action, prompted by a Bombay High Court directive, aims to hold builders accountable for failing to provide promised water infrastructure, forcing residents to rely on private tankers.

news.title: Varkala civic body cracks down on illegal cliffside constructions
news.para: Varkala municipality is cracking down on illegal constructions at North Cliff.  Authorities found many resorts and homestays violating building rules.  Action is being taken against structures built without permits.  The municipality warns of demolition if violations are not rectified.  This drive aims to protect the sensitive coastal area.  Illegal construction often increases during election periods.

news.title: CAQM tightens construction norms to curb dust pollution in Delhi NCR
news.para: According to a 2026 study, dust is a major contributor to pollution, around 15% in winter and 27% in summer. Keeping this in view, the CAQM has issued new directions to control dust and improve air quality.

news.title: India's luxury promise hits a wall: Not enough malls to shop in
news.para: India's booming economy and growing affluent population are driving demand for luxury goods, but a severe shortage of high-quality retail space is hindering brand expansion. Despite significant economic momentum, the luxury market remains underdeveloped due to limited prime mall locations and high import duties, forcing some brands to explore franchise models.

news.title: China's largest banks' profits virtually flat as property slump continues
news.para: China's largest banks reported nearly flat annual profits as the economy grapples with a deepening property crisis. Despite steady net interest margins, analysts warn of risks from Middle East conflict and further margin shrinkage. A significant repricing of high-cost time deposits offers a potential boost to bank profitability.

news.title: Bombay HC panel report on MHADA 79A puts Mumbai's 13,000 unsafe buildings at crossroads
news.para: A Bombay High Court committee has submitted its report on controversial notices for building redevelopment. The court's decision will impact over 13,000 unsafe buildings and lakhs of residents. This ruling could determine if stalled projects resume before the monsoon. The section aims to speed up redevelopment of old structures. Tenants and landlords have presented their cases to the panel.

news.title: Average US long-term mortgage rate leaps to 6.38%, the highest level in more than six months
news.para: American home loan interest rates have surged to their highest point in over six months. This rise is making it more expensive for people to buy homes. The 30-year fixed mortgage rate is now 6.38%. This is happening during the peak home buying season. Higher oil prices and inflation worries are contributing factors. Borrowing costs for refinancing also increased.

news.title: Amravati makes cool roofs mandatory to beat extreme heat
news.para: Amravati Municipal Corporation mandates cool roofs for new buildings and offers tax incentives for existing ones to combat extreme heat and reduce energy demand. This initiative, integrating heat mitigation into urban planning, aims to lower indoor temperatures and enhance climate resilience. The policy follows a study revealing high surface temperatures in urban areas.
